plan_section: Step 3: Delegate mobile/portrait overlay polish and site-wide logo update to Opus UI

plan_alignment.notes: The staged changes implement the planned mobile portrait sound bottom sheet, compact topbar behavior, analyzer/focus/minimal collision handling, and same-origin Colorado Mesh logo replacement without vendor/CoreScope edits.

correctness_safety.notes: Sound API/localStorage contracts are preserved, the sheet supports trigger/Escape/backdrop/close dismissal, focus mode closes/hides the sheet, and runtime logo references are same-origin local assets. No remote logo URLs or secret exposure were found.

code_quality.notes: The overlay changes are localized, readable, and maintain idempotent DOM construction. CSS uses scoped classes, safe-area variables, dynamic viewport units, and explicit mobile breakpoints.

completeness.notes: Acceptance coverage is represented in updated Playwright tests for mobile sheet geometry, open/close behavior, analyzer/focus behavior, safe-area padding, touch-target sizing, logo paths, and metadata icon assets. Provided verification reports targeted Playwright, lint, typecheck, and portrait harness checks passing.

patterns.notes: The implementation stays in the overlay/public asset/site metadata layers, follows the existing constants-based branding pattern, and avoids changing CoreScope vendor files or sound engine semantics.

integration.notes: Imports/exports remain consistent, manifest/metadata/JSON-LD/BrandMark paths align with the new assets, and the overlay topbar/sheet behavior integrates with existing shell modes and sound controls.
